overview.short: Pin Valley National Park is a National park of India located in the Spiti Valley of Lahaul and Spiti district and Bhaba Valley of Kinnaur district, in the state of Himachal Pradesh
overview.description: Pin Valley National Park is a National park of India located in the Spiti Valley of Lahaul and Spiti district and Bhaba Valley of Kinnaur district, in the state of Himachal Pradesh. It is located in far northern India. It is part of Cold Desert (biosphere reserve).

topPlaces.name: Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Himachal Pradesh, India. It is situated near the banks of the Tirthan river.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pin Valley National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pin Valley National Park is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pin Valley National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kullu-Manali Airport (Bhuntar) (53 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (124 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pin Valley National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pin Valley National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pin Valley National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Pin Valley National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pin Valley National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary.
